stories.title: U.S. and Iran fail to reach agreement during high-level talks in Islamabad
stories.description: The U.S and Iran did not reach an agreement to end the war in Iran at a high-level meeting on Saturday.

stories.title: A Hezbollah commander describes battling Israel in Lebanon
stories.description: In a rare interview, a wounded Hezbollah commander tells NPR about his secretive Shia Muslim militia's new command structure and how it has managed to keep firing rockets into northern Israel.

stories.title: Trump claims U.S. will close Strait of Hormuz as Iran peace talks collapse
stories.description: After talks between the U.S. and Iran collapsed, President Trump claimed the U.S. will "blockade" the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Pakistan hosts historic face-to-face talks between the US and Iran
stories.description: In Pakistan's capital, Islamabad, officials from the US and Iran met to discuss a possible end to the war that's engulfed much of the Middle East.

stories.title: What's it like to cover the Ukraine war as the world's attention is focused on Iran?
stories.description: In this week's Reporter's Notebook conversation, we take a closer look at what it's like to cover the war in Ukraine, and how the war in Iran is changing that.

stories.title: After Iran talks falter, the big question is what happens next?
stories.description: Twenty-one hours was not enough to end 47 years of hostility between Iran and the US, writes the BBC's Lyse Doucet.

stories.title: We spoke to the man making viral Lego-style AI videos for Iran. Experts say it's powerful propaganda
stories.description: "Slopaganda" is too weak a term to capture how powerful this "highly sophisticated" content is, one expert says.

stories.title: Concerns after satellite provider restricts Iran images following US pressure
stories.description: Planet Labs says it is restricting coverage of Iran and parts of the Middle East indefinitely.

stories.title: Lebanon and Israel officials to meet in US on Tuesday
stories.description: A senior Lebanese official earlier told the BBC that Lebanon would only take part in talks if a ceasefire was in place.

stories.title: Historic Vance-Ghalibaf talks must bridge deep distrust
stories.description: Face-to-face talks would mark the highest-level engagement between Iran and the US since the 1979 Islamic Revolution.

stories.title: Trump says Iran's handling of Strait of Hormuz is 'not the agreement we have'
stories.description: The US president accused Iran of "doing a very poor job" with the waterway as Israel and Lebanon to begin peace talks.

stories.title: 'Endless fears': Even if fighting stops, the damage to Iran's children will endure
stories.description: The BBC has been able to obtain testimony from parents and those trying to help children deal with the distress that comes with war.

stories.title: Lebanon thought there was a ceasefire - then Israel unleashed deadly blitz
stories.description: Israel says Lebanon is not included in the ceasefire the US agreed to halt the war with Iran.

stories.title: BBC at the site of Israeli air strikes in Beirut
stories.description: At least 182 people were killed and 890 others wounded, according to the Lebanese health ministry.

stories.title: Has US achieved its war objectives in Iran?
stories.description: Key US objectives at the start of the war were to stop Iran getting a nuclear weapon and degrading its arsenal.

stories.title: Iran - Enemies of the Strait: Former NATO General discusses US blockade threats
stories.description: Jean Paul Palomeros, a former French Airforce General, who's held high level command roles in both France and NATO, where he was Supreme Allied Commander in the alliances warfare development command, overseeing transformation from 2012 to 2015.

stories.title: US and Iran fail to reach a deal after peace talks in Islamabad
stories.description: The United States and Iran ended peace talks on Sunday without reaching a deal to end the war, leaving a fragile two-week ceasefire in doubt as both sides blamed each other for the breakdown and signaled little progress on core disputes.

stories.title: How shaky is the Iran-US ceasefire?
stories.description: Will the Iran-US truce lead to peace, or more war?

stories.title: Rescuers dig through rubble after deadly Israeli strikes in south Lebanon
stories.description: Rescuers are digging through rubble after a new wave of Israeli strikes across southern Lebanon killed at least 13.

stories.title: Iranian authorities remain defiant, urge supporters to stay in streets
stories.description: Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, who led Iran's delegation in talks to end the war, said US delegation 'failed to gain trust'.

stories.title: ‘Blown to hell’: Trump orders Hormuz blockade after US-Iran peace talks end
stories.description: US Navy to enforce blockade, Trump says after condemning Iran for blocking waterway, complaining of no deal in Pakistan.

stories.title: FIFA rejects Iran’s request to relocate World Cup games amid US-Israel war
stories.description: Mexican President Sheinbaum says FIFA will not relocate Iran's fixtures from US despite repeated requests.

stories.title: Trump says US to blockade ships crossing Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: US President Donald Trump says the US Navy will immediately blockade the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Lessons from the Iran war
stories.description: The past month and a half have shown that the nature of modern warfare is shifting.

stories.title: US-Iran ceasefire talks: What are the key sticking points?
stories.description: Talks between Iran and the US expose deep divisions, prolonging uncertainty during a two-week ceasefire.

stories.title: UN agency chiefs seek end to war rules violations in Middle East
stories.description: GENEVA: UN agency chiefs on Saturday demanded an end to impunity for widespread international law violations in the Middle East, as casualties pile up six weeks into the war unleashed by US-Israeli strikes on Iran. stories.description: Pakistan has sent a military force and fighter jets to Saudi Arabia to boost security under a joint defence cooperation agreement between the two countries, the Saudi defence ministry said on Saturday. stories.title: Pakistan to continue facilitating U.S.-Iran talks, says Ishaq Dar; urges ceasefire
stories.description: The Pakistan-brokered talks, the first direct, high-level engagement between the two sides since the 1979 Islamic Revolution, were watched globally amid increasing expectations of a breakthrough.
